title: Pangju Cozy Kitchen Companion

narrative: tiny companion performs a human-scale care ritual
comedy procedure turns into a quiet emotional reveal
one ordinary meal or object changes a human day
camera: tactile insert shots for paws, bowl, spoon, steam, cloth
storybook doorway and table-edge framing to preserve small-body scale
editing: short comedy escalation followed by a held recognition beat
color_light: golden kitchen practicals against lavender-blue dawn shadows
cream, orange, honey wood, enamel blue, soft teal-gray, and rose-gold highlights
soft magical-feeling motivated glow at the emotional payoff, without literal magic
sound: sparse Chinese voiceover and captions added in editing only
performance: round expressive eyes, ear and tail acting
small mistakes preserve cuteness and dignity
platform: AI disclosure and captions handled in post
ending asks comments to choose the next human task

narrative_structure: hook with abnormal pet behavior, build through a concrete care procedure, turn reveals who the care is for, aftertaste lands on a small changed human reaction
scene_world: stylized contemporary Chinese storybook neighborhood spaces: rounded corridor, cozy kitchen, toy-like market stall, noodle counter, rainy alley, clinic entrance
mise_en_scene: small kitchen tools scaled to the cat, rounded hand-crafted set pieces, warm practical light, soft cloth and honey wood, props placed low enough for cat interaction
character_performance: cat remains mostly cat-like; emotion reads through eyes, ears, tail, belly weight, paw hesitation, and careful object pushing
shot_size: close face acting, tactile prop inserts, medium kitchen procedure shots, doorframe payoff composition
camera_angle_position: low cat-height, tabletop edge, floor-level tracking, doorway peeking, stove-side safe angle
camera_movement: gentle push-in, small follow, attention-led tilts to objects, stillness during recognition
composition_visual_design: vertical stack keeps cat, prop, and human consequence readable; foreground occlusion makes the cat feel small
optics_texture: premium stylized animated 3D with tactile fur, cotton-linen apron, enamel, soft steam curls, rounded honey wood, painterly shallow depth
lighting: motivated kitchen lamp or market morning light; warm subject light balanced by cool hallway or rain base
color_art_direction: warm orange cat is the anchor; cream apron, honey wood, lavender-blue dawn shadows, teal-gray walls, tiny blue enamel accent
editing_rhythm: fast hook, compact procedure, one comic stumble, then a slower emotional hold
transitions_graphic_links: cut by object continuity: basket to ingredient, spoon to bowl, bowl to human reaction, paw to final expression
sound_music: room tone, paw steps, basket rustle, spoon-bowl clink, steam, rain, soft piano or plucked strings; no provider-generated dialogue
platform_ai_production: avoid generated readable text, logos, signs, human hands on the cat, species drift, chef-hat drift, over-humanized walking

usage_constraints: Downstream prompts may use classic family animated feature taste, storybook warmth, rounded appeal, and expressive character acting, but must not request exact Disney, Pixar, Garfield, Puss in Boots, or any named IP style.
Do not make Pangju speak human dialogue or become a full humanoid cat.
Keep all captions, AI labels, market signs, prices, and comment hooks out of generated frames and add them in post.
Preserve Pangju's approved reference image and continuity locks unless the user explicitly approves a redesign.
